lede: A look at October 15 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Christian, Crown Prince of Denmark and Jessie Ware, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Kidnapping of Jayme Closs
events.desc: 13-year-old American girl, Jayme Closs, is kidnapped from her Barron, Wisconsin home after her parents were both murdered.

events.title: Montreal Protocol
events.desc: One hundred and ninety-seven nations amend the Montreal Protocol to include a phase-out of hydrofluorocarbons.

events.title: Oh-My-God particle
events.desc: The "Oh-My-God particle", an ultra-high-energy cosmic ray measured at 40,000,000 times that of the highest energy protons produced in a particle accelerator, is observed at the University of Utah HiRes observatory in Dugway Proving Ground, Utah.

events.title: Black Panther Party
events.desc: The Black Panther Party is created by Huey P. Newton and Bobby Seale.

events.title: Hurricane Hazel
events.desc: Hurricane Hazel devastates the eastern seaboard of North America, killing 95 and causing massive floods as far north as Toronto.

events.title: Air India
events.desc: Tata Airlines (later to become Air India) makes its first flight.

events.title: America (airship)
events.desc: Airship America is launched from New Jersey in the first attempt to cross the Atlantic by a powered aircraft.

events.title: From Hell letter
events.desc: The "From Hell" letter allegedly sent by Jack the Ripper is received by investigators.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: The Union garrison of Glasgow, Missouri surrenders to Confederate forces.

events.title: Napoleon
events.desc: Napoleon begins his exile on Saint Helena in the South Atlantic Ocean.

births.name: Roy Masters (rugby league)
births.line: Australian rugby league coach, journalist, and author

births.name: Edwin O. Reischauer
births.line: Japanese-American scholar and diplomat, United States Ambassador to Japan (died 1990)

deaths.text: Jim Bolger, New Zealand businessman and politician, 35th Prime Minister of New Zealand
